channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Congress’s to-do list grows while on the campaign trail
item.description: As Congress enters a critical stretch to the November elections, a lengthy to-do list of critical funding items is piling in its absence.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Central Park Five sue Trump for defamation over debate comments
item.description: The exonerated Central Park Five sued former President Donald Trump for defamation on Monday over his comments at the recent presidential debate about their wrongful convictions for rape and assault.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who is Lucifer 'Justin Case' Everylove? Meet the presidential candidate who is only on Utah's ballots
item.description: Who is Lucifer "Justin Case" Everylove? Here's how this unaffiliated presidential candidate made it onto the ballots in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KREX 5

item.title: Harris raises more than $220 million in September, outraising Trump 3-to-1
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris’s campaign raised nearly $222 million in September, raising more than three times as much as former President Donald Trump’s campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Ohio officer stands trial in the 2020 shooting death of Andre Hill
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — A white former Ohio police officer has gone on trial Monday in the killing of Andre Hill, a Black man who was shot as he emerged from a garage holding a cellphone nearly four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Texas man whose execution was halted by subpoena set to testify to lawmakers
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man whose execution was halted after a last-ditch maneuver by lawmakers who believe Robert Roberson did not kill his 2-year-old daughter was expected to testify before a state House panel on Monday, four days after he had been scheduled to die by lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The White House public tour is getting an upgrade. Visitors will see, hear and touch more
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jill Biden trained her educator's eye on the White House public tour and on Monday unveiled an updated design to teach visitors more about the mansion's history and events that unfolded in some of its famous rooms.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: White House proposes insurers cover cost of over-the-counter birth control
item.description: The White House on Monday announced a proposal that would require insurers to cover the cost of forms of contraception like over-the-counter birth control pills and condoms under the Affordable Care Act.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: Still using 'LOL'? That could mean you are getting old
item.description: As time has seen several follow-up iterations, including ROFL and LMAO, the original "laugh out loud" acronym is a telltale sign that who you are talking to may be a millennial.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, ABC 4

item.title: Helicopter crash in Houston kills 4, topples radio tower, officials say
item.description: The private helicopter with four people on board, including a child, either struck a cable or the tower, Houston Police Chief J. Noe Diaz said at a news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How does abortion translate? Ballot measures are a challenge for interpreters
item.description: Reproductive rights measures are on the ballots in 10 states after heated debates over how to describe their impact on abortion — and that's just in English. In 388 places across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL WARREN, GEOFF MULVIHILL and PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jury and families likely to get details on Delphi murder scene on Monday
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. — When she dispatched the jury just before noon on Saturday, Special Judge Fran Gull told the Delphi double murder panel that the prosecution’s next witness was likely to offer lengthy testimony, so she was calling it quits for the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Russ McQuaid, NewsNation

item.title: Texas helicopter crash leaves 4 dead, including child, after hitting radio tower
item.description: Authorities say there were multiple fatalities after a helicopter struck a radio tower and crashed in Houston’s Second Ward.
item.dc:creator: Christian Terry, Corley Peel, T.J. Parker, KPRC 2

item.title: 2 Navy aviators declared dead after fighter jet crash in Washington state
item.description: The aviators' names won’t be released until a day after their next of kin have been notified, the Navy said in a statement Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How does abortion translate? Ballot measures challenge interpreters
item.description: Vague or technical terms can be challenging, even more so when it comes to Indigenous languages that have only limited written dictionaries.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California man suspected of being serial rapist also charged with murder, authorities say
item.description: A California man has been charged with the alleged drugging and sexual assault of at least nine women at homes in Hermosa and Redondo Beach.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Multiple people killed after helicopter crashes into radio tower in Houston
item.description: A helicopter crashed into a radio tower Sunday night in Houston, Texas, killing multiple people, including a child. The FAA is leading the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Should the minimum wage be lower for workers who get tipped? Two states are set to decide
item.description: Voters in Arizona and Massachusetts are set to decide whether employers should be able to continue to pay tipped workers such as servers and bartenders a lower minimum wage than non-tipped workers.
item.dc:creator: Kavish Harjai, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Eyewitness video captures frantic efforts to save lives after deadly collapse of dock walkway
item.description: Eyewitness video from the scene of a deadly gangway collapse at a dock in Georgia shows bystanders jumping into action after 20 people plunged into the water off Sapelo Island.
item.dc:creator: Russ Bynum And Emily Wagster Pettus, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Inflation would be higher under Trump: Economists
item.description: The percentage of economists who believe it rose from the Journal’s last survey, which was before both candidates released their major policy proposals.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Dodgers finish off Mets with 10-5 win in NLCS and advance to face Yankees in World Series
item.description: Tommy Edman and Will Smith homered to send Shohei Ohtani into the World Series for the first time, and the Los Angeles Dodgers eliminated the New York Mets with a 10-5 victory in Game 6 of the NL Championship Series.
item.dc:creator: Beth Harris, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Patrick Mahomes and Chiefs stay undefeated, beat 49ers 28-18 in Super Bowl rematch
item.description: The formula might look a bit different with the defense and running game carrying the Kansas City Chiefs. But the results are awfully familiar as the two-time defending champions keep knocking off teams — especially the San Francisco 49ers.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Full list: Meat recalled over listeria concerns shipped to nearly 200 schools
item.description: Nearly 200 schools across the country received shipments of ready-to-eat meat products that have been recalled over possible listeria contamination, according to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: Full list: Meat recalled over listeria concerns shipped to nearly 200 schools
item.description: Nearly 200 schools across the country received shipments of ready-to-eat meat products that have been recalled over possible listeria contamination, according to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Thelma Mothershed Wair, a member of the Little Rock Nine who integrated an Arkansas school, has died
item.description: Thelma Mothershed Wair, one of the nine Black students who integrated a high school in Arkansas' capital city of Little Rock in 1957 while a mob of white segregationists yelled threats and insults, has died at age 83.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: President Biden and first lady toast a reimagined White House tour that she's unveiling on Monday
item.description: President Joe Biden and first lady Jill Biden on Sunday toasted the unveiling of a long-awaited reimagined public White House tour.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jayden Daniels to get further tests Monday after leaving the Commanders' game with a rib injury
item.description: Jayden Daniels will undergo further tests Monday after leaving the Washington Commanders’ game against the Carolina Panthers with a rib injury, coach Dan Quinn said, repeatedly adding he had no further update on the rookie quarterback's status.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump overtakes Harris for first time in Decision Desk HQ/The Hill election forecast
item.description: CORRECTION: An earlier version of this story misstated the chances of Harris winning the election in the latest forecast from Decision Deck HQ/The Hill. The correct number is 48 percent.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Asheville woman discovers home burglarized after fleeing hurricane
item.description: "They took all [my husband's] power tools that were light and a bunch of different equipment," Jacqui Fehl said.
item.dc:creator: Harrison Grubb, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security benefits: What young people should know
item.description: The Social Security Administration continually tries to remind people of all ages that it pays benefit to more than just retirees.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Texas' junk science law is getting another look over Robert Roberson's case
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — When Robert Roberson's execution was abruptly halted in Texas, it was due to a subpoena ordering him to testify over a legal backstop that both Republicans and Democrats say should had saved him long ago: Texas’ junk science law.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Singer among victims in couple's alleged Las Vegas theft spree during Super Bowl week
item.description: A married couple allegedly stole hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of valuables as Las Vegas hosted the Super Bowl, with one theft involving singer Jason Derulo.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KRON4

item.title: Alec Baldwin returns to 'Saturday Night Live' to spoof Bret Baier interview with Kamala Harris
item.description: Alec Baldwin has returned to “Saturday Night Live.” But the actor who won an Emmy for playing former President Donald Trump didn't reprise that role — he returned Saturday night to play Fox News' Bret Baier in an opening skit that spoofed this week's interview with Kamala Harris.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Oscar forms in the Caribbean
item.description: The formation of Oscar and Nadine comes as parts of the U.S. have been hit hard by a pair of hurricanes, Helene and Milton, that devastated communities, destroyed roads, caused power outages, took lives and injured many.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4
